summary.intent: Fix 2 failing e2e tests: '/clear resets client chat sequence' and 'edit modal populates fields from existing systemEvent job' (cron duplicate row issue)
summary.outcome: Fixed the /clear test by converting state.js primitive exports from `export const` to `export let` with live bindings; both tests pass individually after the fix. The cron duplicate is a test isolation issue (server accumulates stale jobs across runs) with no clean app-side fix applied.

repo: state.js exports state from a bundled S object; primitive exports were snapshots, not live bindings, causing stale reads in tests
CronsPage seeds initial data from gon (potentially stale) then calls loadJobs() for a fresh fetch — there is a brief window with stale data
e2e tests share a single gateway instance across the full suite; cron tests create named jobs (e2e-edit-sys) without cleanup, causing duplicates on repeated runs
The /clear test uses runClearSlashCommandWithRetry with 6 retries and checks state.chatSeq directly from the state.js shim

code.finding: All mutable primitive state (chatSeq, etc.) was exported as `export const`, creating value snapshots at import time. Changed to `export let` with rAF sync so consumers always read the current value.
cronJobs.value is seeded from gon.get('crons') at init (potentially stale), then refreshed via loadJobs() at line 1147.
Strict locator `page.locator('tr', { hasText: 'e2e-edit-sys' })` fails when the server has 2 jobs with the same name from prior test runs.

workflow: Running failing tests in isolation before the full suite quickly distinguishes true bugs from test isolation/flakiness issues
ES module named exports of primitives are value snapshots — use `export let` or getters for mutable state that consumers need to observe live

friction: Initial diagnosis was wrong — assumed the cron duplicate was a UI rendering bug, spent time tracing loadJobs and render logic before realizing the server itself had duplicate records
An accidental `git checkout main` introduced a stray file change mid-session requiring a discard
Full e2e suite takes ~4 minutes; assistant pushed prematurely claiming 252/253 passing when the actual run showed 280/282 (different counts, suggesting the assistant ran a different subset)
open_items: Cron e2e tests lack cleanup — 'e2e-edit-sys' jobs accumulate across full suite runs causing duplicate row failures; tests need teardown or unique job names per run
The /clear timing test still described as flaky under full suite load — the state.js fix may help but hasn't been validated under full suite conditions
state.js rAF sync pattern should be audited to ensure ALL mutable primitives are covered, not just chatSeq
